data.title: Ultragenyx sees FY25 revenue between $672M and $674M
data.description:
• Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) on Monday said total revenue for 2025 is estimated to be $672 million to $674 million, representing approximately 20% growth versus 2024. 
  • Consensus for FY25 revenue is $655.90M.
  • Crysvita revenue for 2025 is estimated to be $480 million to $482 million and represents approximately 17% growth versus 2024.
  • Dojolvi revenue for 2025 is estimated to be $95 million to $97 million and represents approximately 9% growth versus 2024.
  • RARE +3.38% at $23.27.
  • Press Release

data.title: Dynavax gains on disclosure another bidder interested after deal announced
data.description:
Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) rose 1.9% after a disclosure that another bidder was interested in the company after it agreed to be sold to Sanofi (SNY) last month. 

Dynavax (DVAX) shares traded through the $15.50 a share deal with Sanofi (SNY) after a disclosure that unidentified "Party A" appeared to be still interested in a deal on Dec. 28, after the deal was announced on Dec. 24. 

"On December 28, 2025, the Chief Executive Officer of Party A sent an unsolicited email to Mr. Spencer asking Mr. Spencer to speak before Party A determined how to proceed," according to a proxy filing on Monday. "In accordance with the Merger Agreement, Mr. Spencer did not respond to the email. Mr. Spencer informed the Board of the receipt of the email.

Sanofi (SNY) last month  agreed to acquire Dynavax (DVAX) for approximately $2.2 billion, a deal at $15.50 per share in cash, representing a 39% premium to its Dec. 23 closing price.

data.title: Axogen sees Q4 net sales of up to $59.9M and FY2025 at $225.2M
data.description:
• Axogen (AXGN) stated on Monday that preliminary Q4 revenue is expected to be approximately $59.9M, up 21.3% compared to Q4 2024, driven by strong performance across its product portfolio.
  • The company expects FY25 revenue of about $225.2M, a 20.2% increase compared with 2024.
  • Gross margin for Q4 and FY25 is projected above 74%, including one-time costs of roughly $1.9M, representing about 3% of Q4 and 1% of full-year revenue.
  • About 67% of one-time costs are non-cash, related to stock awards tied to FDA BLA approval milestones for Avance.
  • On December 3, 2025, the FDA approved the BLA for Avance.
  • The company anticipates a year-end 2025 cash position of approximately $45.5M, up $6M compared with Dec. 31, 2024.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 net sales are $223.32M and $58M.
  • Shares -2.06%.

data.title: PTC Therapeutics projects FY26 product revenue between $700M to $800M
data.description:
• PTC Therapeutics (PTCT) on Monday reported unaudited 2025 product and royalty revenue of ~$823.4 million, exceeding guidance, and unaudited 2025 product revenue of ~$587.8 million.
  • PTC expects to report ~$235.7 million of full-year 2025 royalty revenue associated with Evrysdi.
  • It posted unaudited Sephience Q4 2025 net revenue of ~$92.5 million, including $81.6 million in the US and $10.9 million ex-US whereas for 2025 the revenue was ~$112.1 million.
  • The company said 1,134 patient start forms were received in the US as of December 31, 2025. Additional Sephience launches are expected in 2026, including in Japan, Brazil and other geographies. 
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of ~$1.94 billion as of December 31, 2025.
  • 2026 Financial Guidance For the full year 2026, PTC anticipates:
    • Total product revenue of $700 to $800 million, excluding Evrysdi royalty revenue and collaboration revenue.
    • GAAP R&D and SG&A expense of $775 to $815 million.
    • Non-GAAP R&D and SG&A expense of $680 to $720 million, excluding estimated non-cash, stock-based compensation expense of $95 million.
  • PTCT is -2.93% to $75.26.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boot Barn issues positive guidance update on strong western boots, denim demand
data.description:
Boot Barn (BOOT) traded higher on Monday after the retailer issued preliminary FQ3 results that exceeded expectations. 

Net sales are projected to be up 16% year over year to approximately $705.6M, driven by consolidated same-store sales growth of about 5.7%, including roughly 3.7% growth in retail stores and 19.6% growth in e-commerce, reflecting broad-based strength across channels and categories. Income from operations is expected to be about $114.8 million, up from $99.5 million a year earlier, with merchandise margin expanding by 110 basis points, helped by buying scale, supply chain efficiencies, and higher penetration of exclusive brands.

 EPS for the quarter is estimated at $2.79 vs. $2.58 consensus and $2.43 in the prior-year period, with the prior year including a $0.22 benefit tied to the former CEO’s equity and bonus forfeiture, implying stronger underlying EPS growth in the current quarter. Management noted that sales momentum accelerated into early January, positioning the company to deliver a strong finish to the fiscal year ahead of formal fourth-quarter guidance on February 4.

Notably, during FQ3, men’s and ladies' western boots had a high single-digit comparable sales growth rate, and the denim category was up in the mid-teens rate. 

Weighing in on the Boot Barn (BOOT) update, BTIG analyst Janine Stichter noted that while comparable sales decelerated sequentially, they accelerated on a 2-year stack, and management continued to generate merchandise margin expansion via ongoing private brand growth and economies of scale and buying efficiencies.

BTIG's bullish stance on Boot Barn (BOOT) is that it is one of the last remaining unit growth stories in retail, while also offering a level of sales and margin consistency that should be afforded a premium in the current market. Boot Barn (BOOT) is seen growing units in the solid double digits annually for the foreseeable future, with the potential to more than double the store base. "At the same time, BOOT's heavily need-based assortment and lack of fashion/seasonal risk offer a degree of relative stability not seen at most retailers, allowing for historically steady performance even in volatile environments," advised Stichter.

Shares of Boot Barn (BOOT) were up 1.3% in late Monday morning action.

data.title: CACI International secures $416 million contract from U.S. Navy
data.description:
• CACI International (CACI) (NYSE: CACI) announced on Monday that it has secured a five-year, single-award task order valued at up to $416 million to provide technology to the U.S. Navy’s Naval Sea Systems Command (NAVSEA) Navy Maritime Maintenance Enterprise Solution (NMMES) program. 
  • CACI will leverage its comprehensive knowledge in Industry 4.0 technologies and Agile software development to modernize and optimize the Navy’s IT infrastructure and applications. 
  • John Mengucci, CACI President and CEO, said, “Modernizing the Navy's IT systems empowers them to make data-driven decisions, optimize resource allocation, and quickly implement critical capabilities to protect our coasts. Our deep understanding of the Navy's specific needs, coupled with a proven past performance, positions us to provide exceptional value to this critical mission.”
  • CACI is +1.4509% to $607.63.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HUB Cyber Security appoints Limor Zur-Stoller as CFO
data.description:
• HUB Cyber Security (HUBC) announced on Monday the appointment of Limor Zur‑Stoller as chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • The company said Limor Zur‑Stoller has held CFO and senior finance roles across cybersecurity, biotechnology, agro-tech, and advanced manufacturing companies, leading finance organizations through periods of scale, restructuring, and strategic transformation.
  • As CFO, Zur-Stoller will lead HUB’s global finance organization, overseeing financial reporting, capital markets activities, treasury, budgeting and controls, and operational finance, it added.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Akebia therapeutics announces corporate updates and 2026 pipeline outlook
data.description:
• Akebia Therapeutics (AKBA) on Monday announced key corporate updates associated with its Vafseo commercial business and provided an outlook on upcoming milestones, including for its next anticipated growth driver, Akebia’s mid-stage rare kidney disease pipeline.
  • The company said Vafseo's total number of prescribers in Q4 was ~785, an increase of 8% over Q3.
  • At least 25% of new patients in Q4 came from dialysis organizations other than U.S. Renal Care (USRC), an increase from less than 10% in Q3.
  • Akebia believes underlying patient dosing demand for Vafseo in Q4 2025 was between approximately $10.5 and $11.5 million.
  • It expects revenue growth to resume in Q1 2026 from increased patient access as well as anticipated improvement in adherence and compliance. 
  • AKBA is -8.88% to $1.385.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Friedland Enterprises takes 5% stake in Lulu’s Fashion Lounge, pushes governance reforms
data.description:
Friedland Enterprises disclosed it has acquired a 5% stake in Lulu’s Fashion Lounge Holdings (LVLU), whose stock has risen roughly 20% following the announcement, and is calling for immediate governance reforms to unlock shareholder value.

Founder Christian Friedland, former Build.com CEO, cited LVLU’s undervaluation despite two consecutive quarters of positive Adjusted EBITDA and improved gross margins, noting the stock trades at just 0.05x revenue versus Revolve Group’s (RVLV) 1.35x.

Friedland’s eight-point plan includes reducing authorized shares from 250M to 10M, reconstituting the board with e-commerce experts, suspending cash board compensation until stock hits $10, and implementing performance-based equity incentives.

Operational changes call for returning founder Colleen Winter to the board, hiring a full-time chief financial officer, and overhauling investor relations.

The plan also emphasizes transparency and strategic alternatives, proposing quarterly investor presentations with key performance metrics and forming a special committee to explore potential sale, merger, or take-private options. Friedland stressed his intent is constructive engagement to support the company’s turnaround.Source: SEC filing

data.title: UBS opposes Swiss proposal to tighten banking rules after Credit Suisse collapse - report
data.description:
UBS (UBS) on Monday said it opposes the Swiss government's proposals to tighten capital rules that are intended to prevent a collapse like Credit Suisse's failure in 2024, saying that they would hurt its ability to compete globally.

The bank argued that Switzerland's banking laws are sufficient, and that the proposed changes wouldn't address the weaknesses like the ones exposed by the Credit Suisse collapse.

"Switzerland already has one of the strictest regulatory capital regimes," the largest Swiss bank said in statements and slides posted on its website. "Under these proposals, UBS would have at least 50% higher capital requirements than its competitors, adversely affecting UBS's business activities in Switzerland and abroad, ultimately undermining the international competitiveness of the Swiss financial center and in turn the Swiss economy."

Even with UBS's (UBS) low-risk business model, the bank already has the highest capital requirements among its peers. "The proposed measures would make it a pronounced outlier," it added.

The proposals would increase UBS minimum CET1 ratio requirements at ~19%, compared with its current de-facto minimum of ~14%, the company said. By contrast, the average minimum CET1 ratio requirement for its peers is 11.5%; the peers' range spans from 8.5% to 13.5%.

UBS (UBS) stock rose 0.2% in Monday morning trading.
